title: helianthemum cinereum subsp. guadiccianum
intro: Helianthemum cinereum subsp. guadiccianum, also known as dwarf rockrose or Sardinian rockrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and other conditions. The health benefits of Helianthemum cinereum subsp. guadiccianum include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
